description: Length-matched V2: Clay Millennium Prize Problems - statements, partial results, and known approaches.

facts.context: The Riemann Hypothesis concerns the zeros of the Riemann zeta function ζ(s). It states that:
facts.truth: all non-trivial zeros have real part 1/2
distractors: all non-trivial zeros have integer real part
non-trivial zeros cluster near real part 0
zeros with large imaginary part drift off-line

facts.context: Computational verification of the Riemann Hypothesis has confirmed:
facts.truth: over 10 trillion zeros lie on the critical line
distractors: a counterexample was found near the trillionth zero
roughly one thousand zeros checked lie on the line
numerical methods cannot reliably locate zeta zeros

facts.context: If the Riemann Hypothesis is true, the prime counting function satisfies:
facts.truth: π(x) = Li(x) + O(√x log x), the sharpest possible error bound
distractors: π(x) = Li(x) + O(x^{2/3}), a weaker bound than currently known
π(x) = x/log x exactly, with no correction term required
π(x) deviates from Li(x) by O(x) for sufficiently large x

facts.context: The P versus NP problem asks whether:
facts.truth: every problem verifiable in polynomial time is also solvable in polynomial time
distractors: every problem solvable in exponential time is also verifiable in linear time
problems solvable by nondeterministic machines require exponential space always
polynomial-time verification implies the existence of subexponential algorithms

facts.truth: any proof separating P from NP cannot relativize to all oracles
distractors: standard diagonalization techniques suffice to separate P from NP
P versus NP is formally independent of Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory
algebraic proof methods can resolve the P versus NP question fully

facts.context: The natural proofs barrier (Razborov-Rudich 1997) establishes that:
facts.truth: if one-way functions exist, natural proof strategies cannot separate P from NP
distractors: if one-way functions exist, diagonalization methods resolve P versus NP directly
natural proof strategies work regardless of cryptographic assumption strength
the existence of pseudorandom generators is irrelevant to circuit lower bounds

facts.context: The Navier-Stokes existence and smoothness Millennium Problem asks whether:
facts.truth: smooth solutions to 3D Navier-Stokes exist globally or singularities can form
distractors: weak solutions to 2D Navier-Stokes remain bounded under turbulent conditions
numerical simulations of 3D Navier-Stokes converge to steady-state solutions
viscosity eliminates all singular behavior in 3D Navier-Stokes automatically

facts.context: For 2D Navier-Stokes, global existence and regularity:
facts.truth: is proven: smooth solutions exist for all time (Ladyzhenskaya 1969)
distractors: remains open: no proof of global regularity exists for 2D flows
is false: finite-time blowup occurs in 2D for large initial data
is conditional: global existence depends on a smallness assumption

facts.context: The Hodge Conjecture asserts that for smooth projective algebraic varieties:
facts.truth: every Hodge class is a rational linear combination of classes of algebraic cycles
distractors: every de Rham cohomology class arises as the pullback of a universal bundle class
only integral cohomology classes correspond to the fundamental classes of subvarieties
Hodge classes on abelian varieties of dimension four are always absolutely decomposable

facts.context: The Birch and Swinnerton-Dyer Conjecture relates:
facts.truth: the rank of an elliptic curve to the vanishing order of its L-function at s=1
distractors: the torsion subgroup of an elliptic curve to the residue of its zeta function
the conductor of an elliptic curve to the leading Taylor coefficient at s=1/2
the discriminant of an elliptic curve to the functional equation of its L-series

facts.context: Partial results on BSD: the conjecture is proven for elliptic curves:
facts.truth: of analytic rank 0 or 1, by Gross-Zagier and Kolyvagin
distractors: of analytic rank 2 or higher, by Bhargava and Shankar
over finite fields only, by the Weil conjectures framework
with complex multiplication only, by Deuring and Shimura

facts.context: The Yang-Mills existence and mass gap problem asks to prove that:
facts.truth: quantum Yang-Mills theory exists in 4D and has a positive mass gap
distractors: quantum Yang-Mills theory exists in 4D and has a vanishing mass gap
classical Yang-Mills equations admit global smooth solutions in 4D
quantum Yang-Mills theory is well-defined only in two dimensions
